data.title: Bitcoin's Rising Value Sparks Renewed Interest in Crypto Lending
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin's price surge has left investors contemplating whether to sell their holdings or continue to hold for potential future gains. This dilemma has reignited interest in crypto lending, a concept that allows investors to access cash without selling their Bitcoin, thus retaining their investment in the digital asset. While crypto lending offers a way to unlock liquidity, it also carries risks, as evidenced by the collapse of several major lending platforms during the last market downturn, which resulted in significant financial losses for customers.

In 2025, the crypto lending landscape is evolving with new companies, innovative approaches, and changing regulations. Decentralized finance (DeFi) protocols are gaining traction, centralized platforms are enhancing their security measures, and institutional interest is quietly increasing. Despite these developments, the question remains whether the current environment is safer for investors or if they are facing similar risks as before. The resurgence of crypto lending is driven by these changes, but potential investors must carefully consider the risks and benefits before engaging in this strategy.

data.title: Ethereum Sees Contract Boom In 2025, Setting Stage For $5,000 Rally
data.description:
Although Ethereum (ETH) failed to break the $5,000 mark on August 24 – pulling back from a new all-time high (ATH) of $4,956 – the second-largest cryptocurrency by market cap may soon cross that milestone, driven by booming new contract activity. Ethereum New Contract Activity Booming – Will Price Follow? According to a CryptoQuant Quicktake post by contributor PelinayPA, a sharp rebound in Ethereum contracts could be seen in 2024 and 2025. This year specifically, new contracts surged dramatically as ETH price climbed beyond $4,500. The CryptoQuant contributor highlighted that during the 2016-17 market cycle, new contract activity remained relatively muted
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/ethereum-news/ethereum-contract-boom-2025-5000-rally/

data.title: Frax Finance Founder Joins New Blockchain Project as CTO

data.title: Bitcoin Price Prediction by Veteran Peter Brandt Amid Crash Concerns
data.description:
Key Insights: Bitcoin price crash concerns mounted after massive sell orders from whales, institutions, and short-term holders. In his Bitcoin price prediction, veteran trader Peter Brandt stressed on the requirement of careful analysis due to bearish reversal patterns. He added that BTC could crash if it fails to reclaim the $117,570 level
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/08/28/bitcoin-price-prediction-by-veteran-peter-brandt-amid-crash-concerns/

data.title: Eliza Labs Sues X Corp Over Alleged Misuse of AI Technology
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, software development company Eliza Labs has filed a lawsuit against Elon Musk's X Corp, accusing it of acquiring crucial technological information before suspending Eliza's account and launching a similar AI product.

Eliza and its founder, Shaw Walters, claim that X Corp induced them to share technical details about AI agents operating on social platforms and coerced developers into paying high enterprise licensing fees to continue their operations.

The lawsuit alleges that X Corp abused its dominant position in social media to suppress competition, violating antitrust laws. Eliza asserts that their removal from the platform was not a publishing decision but a "carefully orchestrated and deceitful" act that harmed the company's relationships with clients and restricted its growth. The case is currently being heard in a federal court in San Francisco, with representatives from both X Corp and Eliza yet to comment on the matter.

data.title: Crypto Bull Run Dead? Analyst Says The Real Top Isn’t Here Yet
data.description:
Top analyst Miles Deutscher says the crypto market’s apparent fatigue is being misread. In a new video titled “Why The Crypto Bull Run Is Far From Over (Data Says This Happens Next),” the commentator—who has more than 630,000 followers on X—argues that both macro and market-structure signals point to an extended cycle, with Ethereum poised to lead even if Bitcoin cools. Crypto Cycle Dead? Deutscher opens by cutting against a swelling narrative that Bitcoin “has potentially put in a top,” acknowledging that spot price action “objectively looks quite weak at the moment. ” Yet, he stresses, “I don’t believe the cycle is over,” and lays out what he considers the telltale sign of a real top—one that he says has not materialized
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/crypto-bull-run-dead-real-top-isnt-here-yet/

data.title: Major stock jumps around crypto moves raise suspicions of insider activity
data.description:
MEI Pharma’s stock jumped in mid-July. It wasn’t because of a new cancer drug. The company said it would buy $100 million of Litecoin for its cash reserves. After that, the share price went from $3 to almost $7. What was odd is that the stock had already risen in the days before the news, […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/major-stock-jumps-around-crypto-moves-raise-suspicions-of-insider-activity/
